name: Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail
name_original: Cerro de la Cruz y Salto del Caballo – circular desde Almedinilla
description: Hike the moderate 5.0-mile Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail, featuring panoramic views and a stunning 30-meter waterfall.
region_intro: Hiking around Almedinilla, located in the Subbética region of Cordoba, Spain, features a landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and Mediterranean forests. The Caicena River flows through the area, providing a fresh atmosphere and supporting lush vegetation, including notable waterfalls like Salto del Caballo. The region sits in the foothills of the Sierra de Albayate, contributing to its varied topography and providing sources for local rivers. Trails showcase diverse Mediterranean flora, including holm oaks, gall oaks, and aromatic plants. Best hiking trails around Almedinilla The most popular hiking route is Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) moderate trail that takes about 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views and leads to the 30-meter Salto del Caballo waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salto del Caballo Waterfall – Zurreón Waterfall loop from Almedinilla, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail connects both the Salto del Caballo and Zurreón waterfalls, following accessible paths. Local hikers also love the Zurreón Waterfall – Salto del Caballo Waterfall loop from Almedinilla, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past waterfalls, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Hiking around Almedinilla is defined by olive groves, the Caicena River, and the foothills of Sierra de Albayate. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Almedinilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Almedinilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Almedinilla?
A: Almedinilla offers a variety of hiking opportunities, with over 10 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more moderate treks, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Almedinilla?
A: Hiking in Almedinilla immerses you in a landscape dominated by extensive olive groves and traditional Mediterranean forests. The refreshing Caicena River flows through the area, creating lush riparian zones, and you'll also find yourself in the foothills of the Sierra de Albayate, offering varied topography.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit on a hike in Almedinilla?
A: Yes, Almedinilla is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The most prominent is the spectacular Salto del Caballo, which you can reach via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999375017/cerro-de-la-cruz-and-salto-del-caballo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable waterfall is El Zurreón, often connected with Salto del Caballo on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31526403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Caballo Waterfall – Zurreón Waterfall loop from Almedinilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Almedinilla?
A: Many of Almedinilla's trails are suitable for families, particularly the easy-rated routes. The area's accessible paths and natural beauty, including waterfalls, make for enjoyable outings with children. Consider routes that connect the Salto del Caballo and Zurreón waterfalls for an engaging experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Almedinilla?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Almedinilla. The natural environment, with its river and open spaces, provides a great setting for walks with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Almedinilla?
A: Yes, Almedinilla offers several circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999375017/cerro-de-la-cruz-and-salto-del-caballo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular hike that provides panoramic views and leads to the Salto del Caballo waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall – Salto del Caballo Waterfall loop from Almedinilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy circular path.

Q: What historical or archaeological sites can I explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Almedinilla is rich in history. You can integrate visits to archaeological sites such as the Iberian settlement of "El Cerro de la Cruz" and the Roman town of "El Ruedo" into your outdoor excursions. These are both Sites of Cultural Interest and offer a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almedinilla?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Almedinilla, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended if visiting during this season. Winters are mild, making hiking enjoyable year-round, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Almedinilla?
A: The hiking trails in Almedinilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the waterfalls and the serene atmosphere along the Caicena River, as well as the well-maintained paths through olive groves and Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking areas in Almedinilla?
A: Almedinilla itself offers local amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your hike. For accommodation, you'll find options within the town or in nearby villages, providing a comfortable base for exploring the region's trails.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: The Caicena River, which flows through the area and alongside many trails, features various pools and cascades. The Salto del Caballo waterfall, in particular, is noted as a place where visitors can bathe, offering a refreshing dip during warmer months.

Q: What kind of flora can I expect to see along the trails?
A: The trails showcase diverse Mediterranean flora. You'll encounter holm oaks, gall oaks, olive trees, and almonds, alongside shrubs like broom and gayomba. The area is also rich in aromatic plants such as thyme, lavender, horehound, pennyroyal, and fennel, many of which have traditional medicinal and culinary uses.
tips: The pool formed by this waterfall is a great place to take a bath at the beginning or end of the route, or both.
Spectacular waterfall that is reached by a cattle path that coincides with a path from Roman times. Before reaching the waterfall there are several pools in which you can bathe.

Along the way you will see ruins of the different flour mills (from the 19th century) that this river moved. There came to be up to ten mills along its bed. Today it feeds the ditches that irrigate the fertile orchards in the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead for the Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route located near Almedinilla in the Córdoba province of Spain. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a popular trail near a village, you can typically find parking in or near Almedinilla to access the starting point.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered to be of moderate difficulty, suitable for most skill levels but requiring good fitness. The terrain is varied, including easily accessible paths, mild inclines, and some stairs. Sure-footedness is needed in certain sections as it traverses the Sierra de Almayate.

faqs.question: What natural and historical sights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich variety of sights. Key natural highlights include the impressive Salto del Caballo Waterfall (over 30 meters high), the Zurreón Waterfall, and the scenic Caicena River. Historically, you'll pass an ancient Iberian settlement, Civil War trenches near the hermitage at Cerro de la Cruz, and ruins of 19th-century flour mills along the river.

faqs.question: Can I swim at the Salto del Caballo Waterfall?
faqs.answer: Yes, the pool created by the Salto del Caballo Waterfall is a popular spot for bathing, offering a refreshing break during your hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be hiked at any time of year. However, it's advisable to avoid the hottest parts of the day, especially during summer, to ensure a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or specific leash rules. It's generally recommended to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the Cerro de la Cruz and Salto del Caballo Trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Andalucía - North Route (Provincia de Córdoba) and the Sendero del Salto del Caballo, offering potential for longer excursions or alternative paths.

faqs.question: What kind of vegetation and wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses Mediterranean forest vegetation, including holm oaks, gall oaks, olive trees, and almond trees. You'll also see various shrubs and plants like broom, thyme, and lavender. The numerous small cavities in the geological formations serve as refuges for various birds.

faqs.question: Is the trail well-marked?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally well-maintained, but it is only partially signposted. It's recommended to use a reliable navigation tool, such as the komoot app, to stay on track.

faqs.question: Are there any unique geological features on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail goes near the Tajo de las Llanás, an interesting geological formation of travertine. This sedimentary rock often shows imprints of ancient animals and plants, and the area features numerous small cavities.
